data.title: REG - Kier Group PLC - Transaction in Own Shares - Replacement
data.description:
RNS Number : 5946H Kier Group PLC 14 November 2025  

________________________________________________________________________________

This announcement replaces, in its entirety, the Transaction in Own Shares announcement released on 13 November 2025 at 07.00 a.m. under RNS number 3198H.

The full amended text is shown below.

________________________________________________________________________________

 13 November 2025

 Kier Group plc

 Transaction in Own Shares

KIER GROUP PLC ("Kier", or the "Company") announces that on 12 November 2025 it purchased the following number of its Ordinary shares of 1p each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") from Peel Hunt LLP as part of the buyback programme announced on 21 January 2025 (the "Buyback Programme").

Date of Purchase

12 November 2025

PEEL HUNT

 Kier intends to hold the Ordinary Shares purchased pursuant to the Buyback Programme in Treasury.

 Following the settlement of the above purchases, Kier has purchased a total of 8,424,700 Ordinary Shares since the commencement of the Buyback Programme and will have 452,875,390 Ordinary Shares of 1 pence each in issue. 8,424,700 Ordinary Shares are held in treasury. The figure 444,450,690 represents the total number of voting rights in Kier and can be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

 In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (as such Regulation has become part of domestic law by virtue of and for the purposes of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 (as amended by the European Union (Withdrawal Agreement) Act 2020) and as such Regulation has been amended in such form by any regulations made under that Act), the schedule below contains detailed and aggregated information of the individual trades made by Peel Hunt as part of the buyback programme.

data.title: MDA Space Quarterly Revenue Rises 45% With Increased Satellite, Robotics and Space Work
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

MDA Space logged strong third-quarter revenue growth in the latest quarter and stuck with its guidance for the full year, despite earnings being squeezed by a rise in costs and expenses in the latest quarter.

The Canadian robotics, satellite systems and geointelligence provider's net income slipped to 24.4 million Canadian dollars ($17.4 million), or C$0.19 a share, from C$29.5 million, or C$0.24, a year earlier.

Revenue costs related to materials, labor and subcontractors was up on last year, as were operating expenses related to administration and research and development.

Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization on an adjusted basis rose to C$82.8 million from C$55.5 million last year. Analysts were expecting adjusted Ebitda of C$77.8 million.

Revenue increased 45% for the quarter to C$409.8 million, in line with the C$385 million to C$415 million guidance set by the company. The jump was driven by higher volumes of work in MDA's satellite systems and robotics and space businesses, it said.

MDA was picked in July by the Canadian Space Agency to conduct an early-stage study of a proposed lunar utility vehicle for the country. In late June, the company received a follow-on contract worth about C$1 billion for the next phases of the Canadian Space Agency's Canadarm3 program that will be used aboard the NASA-led effort to establish a space station in lunar orbit that would support missions to the moon.

Canadarm is one of Canada's most famous technical achievements in robotics. The manipulator arm, developed by a number of Canadian companies, was used to support U.S. space shuttle missions for about 30 years following its first deployment in space in late 1981.

MDA said its backlog sits at roughly C$4.39 billion, down by C$185.3 million from the end of September as it continued to work through projects, providing it with revenue visibility for 2025 and beyond.

The company affirmed its financial guidance for the full year, and continues to expect revenue of between C$1.57 billion and C$1.63 billion and adjusted Ebitda of C$305 million to C$320 million, which at the midpoint would mark growth of 45%. It forecasts 2025 cash flow to be neutral to positive.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: EU Conditionally Clears $13.7 Billion Adnoc-Covestro Deal
data.description:
(11:00 GMT) *EU Clears Adnoc's Covestro Deal Under Foreign Subsidies Rules
(11:00 GMT) *EU Approves Adnoc Aquisition of Covestro With Conditions
(11:00 GMT) *EU Adnoc Clearance Follows In-Depth Investigation
(11:18 GMT) EU Conditionally Clears $13.7 Billion Adnoc-Covestro Deal

By Edith Hancock

The European Union's competition regulator conditionally cleared Abu Dhabi's state oil company's $13.7 billion bid for German plastics maker Covestro after an in-depth foreign subsidies probe.

The European Commission said Adnoc's offer to adapt its articles of association to make sure they align with UAE insolvency law-- essentially removing any unlimited guarantee from the state --and to share Covestro's sustainability patents with some competitors would balance out the negative affects it was concerned the transaction could have on the EU's internal market.

"Clear, pre-defined access to these patents will enable others to innovate and advance research in an area that is critical for Europe's future," Teresa Ribera, the bloc's competition commissioner, said on Friday.

Adnoc set out its plan to buy Covestro last year in a deal that would value the company at around 11.7 billion euros ($13.61 billion) and position the combined group as a more fully integrated energy business.

But the acquisition has faced pressure from EU watchdogs, who opened an in-depth investigation earlier this year under a relatively new piece of legislation designed to tackle what European officials see as distorting subsidies from foreign governments.

Write to Edith Hancock at edith.hancock@wsj.com

data.title: Verastem Oncology Shares Fall on $90M Stock Offering
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Shares of Verastem Oncology slid after the company said it would offer 8.54 million shares at a price of $7.25 a share as well as pre-funded warrants for certain investors to purchase up to an aggregate of 3.87 million shares.

The stock fell 8.4% to $7.43 in premarket trading after closing Thursday up 57% this year.

The company said it expects gross proceeds of roughly $90 million from the offering, which is expected to close Monday.

Verastem also granted the underwriters of the offering a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.86 million shares.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.description:
Q2 FY2026 saw 3% sales volume growth and 9% revenue growth, but margins declined due to higher gas costs and sourcing mix changes. Margin and volume growth are expected to improve with VAT reduction, tariff rationalization, and continued CNG vehicle adoption.

data.title: Croatian consumer prices up 3.6% year-on-year in October, led by housing costs
data.description:
Croatian consumer prices rose 3.6% year-on-year in October, confirming an earlier flash estimate, driven mainly by higher housing and utility costs, according to data released by the statistics office on Friday.

Month-on-month, consumer prices edged up 0.6% on average.

Housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels recorded the highest annual increase, climbing 8.4% and contributing 1.42 percentage points to overall inflation. Prices for food and non-alcoholic beverages climbed 4.4%, while those for restaurants and hotels increased 6.9%.

Clothing and footwear was down 2.2%, while transport fell 1.1%.

In comparison, consumer prices in October 2024 were up 2.2% year-on-year.

data.title: BTBT: Q3 2025 saw surging revenue, record net income, and a major pivot to Ethereum staking
data.description:
Q3 2025 revenue rose 33% year-over-year to $30.5 million, with net income of $146.7 million and a strategic shift to Ethereum treasury and staking. ETH holdings and staking yields grew sharply, while bitcoin mining operations are winding down.

Original document: Bit Digital, Inc. [BTBT] SEC 8-K Current Report — Nov. 14 2025

Disclaimer

data.title: Saudi Arabia – Qiddiya HSR PPP EoIs in
data.description:
By Rod Morrison

The Royal Commission for Riyadh City, the National Centre for Privatisation & PPP and Qiddiya Investment Company have received expressions of interest from 145 companies for its Qiddiya high-speed rail PPP project.

Equity investors submitting EoIs include Investcorp, Al Gihaz Holding, Almutlaq Real Estate Investment, Arj HoldingFoure Holdings, Itochu, Korea Overseas Infrastructure & Urban Development, Lamar Holding, MADA International, Meritz Financial, MXB Investment, Plenary, Sojitz, Tamasuk, Vinci Concessions and Vision International Investment.

Rolling stock companies and system suppliers submitting EoIs include Alstom, CAF, Colas Rail, CRRC, Hitachi Rail, Hyundai Rotem, Siemens Mobility, Stadler Rail and AGTalgo.

Rail operators submitting EoIs include Alsa Grupo, Alsaif Transportation, DB International Operations, Ferrovie dello Stato Italiane, Intertoll Europe, Keolis, Moventis, MTR, RATP Dev, Renfe Operadora, Serco and Transdev.

Sixty-eight companies submitted EoIs as lead developers and contractors, including Bouygues Travaux Publics, FCC Construccion, Kalyon Insaat Sanayi ve Ticaret, Larsen & Toubro Construction, Marubeni, Mota-Engil, Orascom Construction, Saipem, Samsung C&T Corporation, UCC Holding, Webuild and Yapi Merkezi Insaat ve Sanayi.

The Q-Express 250kmh scheme will link King Salman International Airport and King Abdullah Financial District with Qiddiya City and will be built in two phases. Journeys from central Riyadh to Qiddiya will take 30 minutes.

data.title: Sterling Could Reverse Falls if U.K. Sticks to Fiscal Prudence — Market Talk
data.description: Sterling could undo its decline against the euro if markets expect that the U.K. will maintain fiscal discipline at the Nov. 26 budget, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. Friday's reports about the government possibly abandoning income tax rises at the budget don't suggest that Treasury Chief Rachel Reeves is "willing to radically change her fiscal prudence commitment," Pesole says. Although the risks of the sterling weakening against the euro have increased, the weakness is likely to be reversed, he says. The euro earlier hit a 2.5-year high versus sterling of 0.8865 pounds, LSEG data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Forecasted Heavy Rain Prompts Postponement of Compassion Center Coalition for Patient Rights Inaugural Hoedown to Dec. 13
data.description:
Outdoor toy-drive event rescheduled to prioritize safety as Las Vegas faces 70-90% chance of rain on Saturday

LAS VEGAS, NEVADA / ACCESS Newswire / November 14, 2025 / In light of forecasts calling for a 70-90 percent chance of rain this Saturday in the Las Vegas valley, Compassion Center's Coalition for Patient Rights (CPR) is postponing its Inaugural Hoedown and Operation Warm Heart toy drive to Saturday, December 13, 2025, at the same location and time.

The Hoedown, originally scheduled as a full outdoor experience with a live sound stage, bounce house, potato sack races, and family-friendly activities, relies on dry conditions to operate safely. With rain expected throughout key event hours and the added risk of slick roads and impaired visibility, organizers determined that moving the date is the most responsible course of action.

"We are deeply grateful to our sponsors, performers, vendors, and volunteers, and we share everyone's disappointment in having to postpone," said James B. Creel, organizer and spokesperson for the Compassion Center Coalition for Patient Rights. "However, it makes no sense to invite families to spend the day outdoors, enjoy food and drinks, and then send them back onto Las Vegas roads in heavy rain. Even a typical rainy day snarls traffic here - adding crowds and alcohol into the mix would be irresponsible. Safety comes first."

The Hoedown and Operation Warm Heart Toy Drive will now take place on Saturday, December 13, 2025, at the same venue and during the same advertised hours. All planned programming - including live music, family activities, community resource booths, and the toy drive benefiting local families - is expected to proceed as originally envisioned with only slight adjustments and additions, weather permitting.

CPR emphasized that sponsor commitments and community support remain central to the event's mission.

"Our sponsors have been nothing short of extraordinary," Creel added. "Their support of patient rights, integrative healthcare, and local families doesn't change just because Mother Nature had other plans. We want to publicly thank each sponsor, vendor, and partner for standing with us as we shift to a safer date in December."

Organizers also stressed that postponing the event is about more than logistics; it is about protecting the very community they serve.

"This Hoedown is about bringing people together around compassion, inclusion, and wholeness," said Creel. "Rescheduling gives us the chance to deliver the experience our community deserves - with a proper stage, safe kids' activities, and a clear, dry trip home at the end of the day."

Updated event details - including confirmation of entertainment lineups, sponsor activations, and Operation Warm Heart toy drive information - will be shared on Compassion Center and Coalition for Patient Rights communication channels in the coming days.

Event Details (Updated)

  • What: Compassion Center Coalition for Patient Rights Inaugural Hoedown & Operation Warm Heart Toy Drive

  • New Date:Saturday, December 13, 2025

  • Time: 10am - Dusk

  • Location: Paradise Recreation Center, 4775 McLeod Dr. Las Vegas, NV 89121

  • Benefiting: Local families and the Operation Warm Heart community toy drive

Community members are encouraged to mark their calendars for the new date and to continue planning to bring a new, unwrapped toy to support families during the holiday season.

About the Compassion Center

The Compassion Center is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to advancing and promoting integrative health and wellness in the local community. Through education, outreach, and support programs, the Compassion Center ensures every individual they touch has access to the tools and resources they need to live healthier, more empowered lives.

To learn more about Compassion Center, please visit: https://compassion-center.org

About the Coalition for Patient Rights

Coalition for Patient Rights (CPR) is a nonpartisan, patient-led advocacy network dedicated to ensuring that healthcare policy and practice remain centered on patient safety, access, and dignity. CPR advances reforms that remove barriers to care, protect civil liberties, and align regulation with real-world science-so patients, providers, and communities can thrive.

CPR's Five Pillars

  • Bring About Regulatory Change - Campaign for state and federal reforms that expand access to high-quality healthcare.

  • Highlight the Inadequacies and Failures of the Current System - Call out restricted treatment choice and other systemic gaps that harm patients.

  • Educate People About the Treatments They Are Given - Increase transparency about what's in medicines and how components may affect the body.

  • Improve the Environment - Advocate for clean water and air as foundations of public health.

  • Safeguard the Use of New Technologies - Ensure healthcare technology and data are used safely, accountably, and for patient benefit.

To learn more about Coalition for Patient Rights, please visit: https://coalitionforpatientrights.org

To learn more about the Coalition for Patient Rights' Events like these, please visit: https://coalitionforpatientrights.org/events

Important Contacts:

Contact: Sarah Schwefel - Executive Director of Fundraising and Advocacy

Email: sarah.schwefel@compassion-center.org

Phone: 702-350-1874

Events: https://coalitionforpatientrights.org/events

SOURCE: Compassion Center

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Execution of share sales under previously resolved option commitment
data.description:
GreenMerc AB (publ)

Stockholm, 14 November 2025 | In accordance with previously entered option agreements, GreenMerc's founders and principal owners, Ari Liukko and Steffan Sondermark, have completed minor sales of shares in GreenMerc AB (publ).

Ari Liukko and Steffan Sondermark have each sold 154,160 B shares. The transactions have been made to members of GreenMerc's advisory board as part of the company's long-term incentive structure. The aim is to further strengthen their commitment and ownership in the company, in line with GreenMerc's strategy to connect key employees to the company's continued development.

The divestments do not affect the company's capital structure, and the founders remain significant owners of GreenMerc AB (publ).

Contact

For further information, please contact:

Arvid Börje Ramberg, arvid@greenmerc.com

About GreenMerc: GreenMerc strives to connect traditional finance and decentralized finance (defi), with the goal of driving growth through the launch of scalable web-based financial services. The company focuses on innovative technology solutions and offers a broad portfolio of services, from development to finished, deployed and managed solutions, enabling a more accessible and transparent financial market for GreenMerc’s customers. GreenMerc is listed on NGM Nordic SME with the ticker GMERC B. Read more at www.greenmerc.com.

About Trijo: Trijo is a subsidiary of GreenMerc and Sweden’s leading crypto platform. Trijo is a pioneer in the Swedish cryptocurrency market and specializes in digital currency services. With an app available for both iPhone and Android, and support for 260+ new currencies, Trijo works to provide easier access to cryptocurrencies. Trijo has also launched a service that enables staking, which yields returns on digital assets. Read more at www.trijo.se.

https://news.cision.com/greenmerc-ab--publ-/r/execution-of-share-sales-under-previously-resolved-option-commitment%2Cc4267340

data.title: HI MOBILITY TO ACQUIRE TWO FIRMS TO BOOST MASS TRAFFIC CAPABILITIES
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 14 (Bernama) — HI Mobility Bhd has entered into two conditional share sale agreements to acquire Handal BCM Sdn Bhd and Acacia Motor Services Sdn Bhd, a move aimed at strengthening its core operational capabilities in the mass traffic sector.

HI Mobility said in a statement that the acquisitions are valued at RM52.9 million for Handal BCM and RM29.63 million for Acacia, to be satisfied through the issuance of 22.04 million and 12.34 million new ordinary shares in HI Mobility at RM2.40 per share.

HI Mobility executive director and chief executive officer Lim Chern Chuen said the acquisitions mark the company's evolution from a service operator to a more integrated commercial vehicle industry player.

"Through the integration of Handal BCM and Acacia, we aim to strengthen our core bus operations through the realisation of meaningful synergies, improve efficiency and establish a more integrated and streamlined transportation value chain, which positions us for sustainable long-term growth," he said.

Lim added that HI Mobility is seeing promising opportunities in the electric vehicle (EV) sector, noting that RM1.9 billion has been allocated to a government-owned public transport company to procure about 1,660 buses, of which 1,350 are electric buses

"We maintain cautious optimism that the consolidation of Handal BCM and Acacia will deliver synergistic advantages, enabling HI Mobility to achieve economies of scale across procurement, logistics and production, alongside the reduction of overheads realised by streamlining overlapping functions.

"This overall financial impact is expected to be earnings accretive to us and enhance shareholder value,” he said.

-- BERNAMA

TAGS: HI Mobility, Handal BCM, Acacia, EV

data.title: CHOW: PENANG TO CONTINUE I-SEJAHTERA INITIATIVE NEXT YEAR WITH ALLOCATION OF RM65 MLN
data.description:
GEORGE TOWN, Nov 14 (Bernama) — The Penang government will continue the i-Sejahtera programme next year, with an estimated allocation of RM65 million, to ensure no group is marginalised in the state.

Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, when tabling its Budget 2026, said the state government had spent a total of RM53.57 million involving 287,923 recipients as of Oct 31.

The i-Sejahtera initiative is proof of the state government's sincerity in caring for vulnerable groups and appreciating the contributions of the people of this state.

"In 2024, the state government distributed a total of RM56.24 million, including funeral management assistance, to 286,335 recipients," he said while tabling the budget at the State Legislative Assembly session here today.

Penang Governor Tun Ramli Ngah Talib, when officiating the First Meeting of the Third Session of the 15th Penang State Legislative Assembly on May 16, opined that the i-Sejahtera programme should be continued with a more targeted and inclusive approach.

This follows Chow's statement on May 9 that Penang is conducting a study and review of the i-Sejahtera programme to ensure it achieves its target.

The i-Sejahtera initiative is a recognition and concern of the state government, contributing financially once a year to the people of Penang, namely the elderly, persons with disabilities, single mothers, housewives and infants.

-- BERNAMA

TAGS: Penang, state assembly, Budget 2026, i-Sejahtera Programme, Chow Kon Yeow

data.title: Kazakhstan GDP Growth Rate at 2011-Highs
data.description:
The economy of Kazakhstan expanded 6.3% year-on-year in January-September 2025, the strongest since 2011, compared to 6.2% in the first half of 2025.

Construction recorded the biggest increase (14.9%), followed by mining and quarrying (9.3%), industry (7.3%), agriculture (4.%) and utilities production (2.1%).
